name: Retry storm

injection.description: Induce failures on one backend while a client fleet retries aggressively (immediate retries, no backoff or jitter), attempting to amplify load through the gateway.

expected_gateway_semantics: The gateway's retry budget caps amplification: gateway-initiated upstream retries never exceed the configured budget fraction of base request rate, regardless of client behavior.
Excess offered load from client retries is handled by admission control (fs-06 semantics: 429 + Retry-After), not forwarded upstream.
All gateway retries remain pre-first-token only.
expected_client_visible_behavior: Storming clients increasingly receive 429 + Retry-After once budgets engage.
Well-behaved clients continue to meet the latency SLO — the storm does not take down healthy capacity.

metrics_that_must_move.expectation: increases, but its rate stays at or below the configured retry-budget fraction of request rate (amplification capped)
increases with reason=retry_budget (and/or reason=queue_full) once budgets engage

metrics_that_must_not_move.expectation: stays 1 for the non-injected backends — retry traffic does not saturate healthy capacity

abort_condition: Abort the injection if upstream request rate exceeds (1 + retry-budget fraction) x offered base rate (amplification uncapped), or if a non-injected backend saturates from retry traffic.
